# no-snapshot-in-loop

📝 Disallow snapshot assertions inside loop bodies.

🚫 This rule is _disabled_ in the following [configs](https://github.com/sindresorhus/eslint-node-test#preset-configs): ✅ `recommended`, ☑️ `unopinionated`.

<!-- end auto-generated rule header -->
<!-- Do not manually modify this header. Run: `npm run fix:eslint-docs` -->

`t.assert.snapshot()` stores snapshots by position. When it runs inside a loop, adding or removing one iteration shifts the index of every following snapshot, which makes unrelated snapshots fail. Prefer one snapshot for the full collection, or generate separate tests with their own titles.

This rule reports direct test and subtest context `t.assert.snapshot()` calls inside `for`, `for…of`, `for await…of`, `for…in`, `while`, and `do…while` loop bodies. It intentionally does not report destructured or computed-property assertion calls, `fileSnapshot()`, snapshot configuration APIs, array callback iteration, snapshots inside nested helper functions or callbacks, or snapshots inside callbacks of tests or subtests generated by a loop.

## Examples

```js
import test from 'node:test';

// ❌
test('items', t => {
	for (const item of items) {
		t.assert.snapshot(item);
	}
});

// ✅
test('items', t => {
	t.assert.snapshot(items);
});

// ✅
for (const item of items) {
	test(`item ${item.id}`, t => {
		t.assert.snapshot(item);
	});
}
```
